title: A Multi-Scale Model of Cardiac Electrophysiology
author: Colleen Clancy
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5nkg5cw

steps.title: Creating multi-scale model of cardiac electrophysiology...
steps.instruction: Creating multi-scale model of cardiac electrophysiology We developed a workflow containing differential equation models of cardiac physiology that automate the execution of simulations with user defined options of outputs from a single cell, 1 or 2D tissue, and a pseudo-ECG output, which can be compared to experimental or clinical data.

steps.title: Using Intel Compiler
steps.instruction: Using Intel Compiler: icc masterCompute.cpp -openmp -o oneD ./oneD initial_WTstates.txt stim_param.txt testOutputFolderTWOd ____________________________________________________________________________________ OR (

steps.title: Using GCC compiler
steps.instruction: Using GCC compiler: g++ masterCompute.cpp -fopenmp -o oneD ./oneD initial_WTstates.txt stim_param.txt testOutputFolderONEd ____________________________________________________________________________________ Input settings in stim_param.txt 1 Set Na blocker drug concentration 2 Set Kr blocker ratio 3 Set basic cycle length (BCL) 4 Set how many beats for 1D (e.g 200). For 2D, set beat = 1 5 Set Ligand concentrations 6 Adjust cAMKII activity levels (expression = 'WT', 'OE', or 'KO')

steps.title: tissue size = tw * tl
steps.instruction: tissue size = tw * tl. Enter value for tw.

steps.title: tissue size = tw * tl
steps.instruction: tissue size = tw * tl. Enter value for tl.

steps.title: homogeneous or heterogeneous ____________________________________________________________________________________ For 1D simulations: Read initial_WTstates
steps.instruction: homogeneous or heterogeneous ____________________________________________________________________________________ For 1D simulations: Read initial_WTstates.txt which is the initial variables file corresponding to SS_rabbit_varNames.txt (total variables are 206, although some of them are not used in the model) (

steps.title: outputs generated in ecgs.txt (1D case) 1st column
steps.instruction: outputs generated in ecgs.txt (1D case) 1st column: time 2nd column: ecg (

steps.title: Time course of AP surface (1D)...
steps.instruction: Time course of AP surface (1D) ____________________________________________________________________________________ For 2D simulations: Read in fiber.cell which is generated from 1D simulations (

steps.title: Time snapshots for 2D In order...
steps.instruction: Time snapshots for 2D In order to compile Single_Cell Human_0D1D2DWF program model,located in Code\Human_0D1D2DWF\ Single_Cell folder in our data set, follow these instructions: To compile the program: Using Intel Compiler ICC icc masterCompute.cpp -o singlecell ./singlecell initial_WTstates.txt stim_param.txt testOutputFolder __________________________________________________________________ Using GCC g++ masterCompute.cpp -o singlecell ./singlecell initial_WTstates.txt stim_param.txt testOutputFolder __________________________________________________________________ plotall.m collects the results from masterCompute.cpp and plots the time course of voltage or currents ____________________________________________________________________________________ Input settings in stim_param.txt Set Na blocker drug concentration Set Kr blocker ratio Set basic cycle length (BCL) Set how many beats. Set Ligand concentration Adjust cAMKII activity levels (expression = 'WT', 'OE', or 'KO') ____________________________________________________________________________________ initial_WTstates.txt is the initial variables file corresponding to SS_rabbit_varNames.txt (total variables are 206, although some of them are not used in the model) __________________________________________________________________________________ (

steps.title: outputs generated in vm_1Hz
steps.instruction: outputs generated in vm_1Hz.txt 29Ca bound to Casqn Ca SR Na dyad Na sl 33Na cytosol K Ca dyad Ca sl Ca cytosol Vm (

steps.title: outputs generated in allresult_1Hz
steps.instruction: outputs generated in allresult_1Hz.txt I_Ca_store, I_to_store[0], I_Na_store, I_K1_store, Jserca, IKs_store, IKr_store, Jleak[0], Jleak[1], ICFTR, pars1.Incx (

steps.title: outputs generated in apds_1Hz.txt 1st column
steps.instruction: outputs generated in apds_1Hz.txt 1st column: beat 2nd column: APD90
